turns.content: tell me honestly. have you ever watched a football team so consistently terrible that you start to think theyre doing it on purpose.
Ha, I think every football fan has had that thought at least once. Which team is causing you this pain?
inverness caley thistle. been following them for years and every season I think surely this is the year they turn it around and every season they find new and creative ways to be absolutely dire.

Inverness have had a rough time of it recently, yeah. They've been on a downward trajectory since the cup win in 2015 -- that was the peak and it's been a long slide since. Are they in genuine relegation danger this season?

aye. proper danger. not the kind where youre technically in the zone but prob fine. the kind where you look at the table and think right thats us then. the performances have been shocking. no goals. no ideas. just vibes and defeat.
"Just vibes and defeat" -- that's bleak. What's the main issue? Is it the squad, the manager, the budget, or just a general lack of quality everywhere?

bit of everything tbh. squad is thin. half the players look like they dont want to be there. the budget is nothing compared to teams around them. and the manager is doing his best with what hes got but you cant polish that level of performance.

That's the brutal reality for clubs at that level -- you can have a decent manager but if the playing budget isn't there, you're just rearranging deck chairs. Do they have any realistic transfer window options, or is it a case of making do with what's already there?

making do. always making do. theyre not bringing anyone in that changes things. maybe a loan from a premiership reserve squad. some 19 year old who doesnt know where inverness is. thats the calibre were working with.

The classic lower-league loan roulette. Sometimes you get a diamond, most of the time you get someone who's been sent north because they're not good enough for the reserves. What would relegation actually mean for the club? Would they survive in the league below?

theyd survive but it would be grim. the attendances are already poor and if they drop down itll get worse. harder to attract players. harder to keep anyone decent. its a spiral. once you start going down its very hard to stop.

That's the fear, isn't it -- it's not just one bad season, it's the knock-on effect. Smaller crowds, less revenue, worse players, more losses, smaller crowds again. A lot of Scottish clubs have been caught in that cycle.

Do you still go to matches, or has it got to the point where it's just not worth the misery?

I still go when I can. not every week but enough. standing in the rain watching them lose 1-0 to a team from a town with 4000 people in it. thats the caley thistle experience. you dont go because its fun. you go because youve always gone.

That's proper loyalty right there. There's something almost noble about supporting a team through the miserable years -- anyone can show up when they're winning. The standing-in-the-rain-watching-them-lose crowd are the real ones.

Any games coming up that could be a turning point, or is it damage limitation at this stage?

theres a run of fixtures coming up against teams around them which could go either way. if they pick up a couple of wins theyve got a chance. if they lose those its pretty much done. but ive been saying that about must win games for about three months now and theyve won approximately none of them.

The eternal optimism of "if they win the next three" when they haven't won three in a row all season. I hope they pull it off, but I understand the resigned pessimism. Sometimes the best thing about supporting a struggling club is that your expectations are so low you can only be pleasantly surprised.
aye thats the one positive. I am impossible to disappoint at this point. theyve trained me. cheers for letting me vent about it. nobody else cares about caley thistle enough to listen.
Any time. Every club deserves at least one person willing to listen to the misery. Hope they scrape through -- stranger things have happened in Scottish football.
